This is standard practice; unless the modules themselves are malicious, this pattern is safe.\n   - No assumptions about vulnerabilities are justified solely from this code snippet.\n\n5. **Consistency of Scores**:\n   - All reports assign very high confidence (close to 1) that this is benign, with zero malware and obfuscation.\n   - The overall assessment aligns with the code's nature.\n\n6. **Additional Considerations**:\n   - Since the code is boilerplate, the main security concern would be the trustworthiness of the external modules.
